hero.headline: Chandigarh Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Harvard and PwC certification options

sections.tldr: Chandigarh Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain features AI-Enabled Demand Forecasting, Smart Logistics and Quick Commerce, PwC-taught modules (premium tier), zero-cost direct university EMI, and 25% Early Bird scholarship at Rs 1,65,000. NIRF #19, NAAC A+. For logistics, e-commerce supply chain, and procurement careers.

about.body:
Logistics and Supply Chain Management is among the most employment-rich MBA specialisations in India, driven by e-commerce logistics expansion, PLI scheme manufacturing growth, and the government's National Logistics Policy targeting a reduction of logistics costs from 13% to 8% of GDP. Chandigarh University Online's LSCM MBA reflects the current logistics landscape with AI-Enabled Demand Forecasting, Smart Logistics and Last-Mile delivery, and Quick Commerce operations.

The programme is designed for working professionals in freight forwarding, e-commerce logistics, manufacturing supply chains, and procurement functions who want a management credential while continuing to work.

For Tier 2 enrollees, PwC's Operations and Supply Chain advisory practice advises clients on end-to-end supply chain transformation, last-mile network design, and supplier risk management - content directly reflected in the co-taught modules.

Chandigarh University holds NIRF #19 overall and NIRF #32 in Management 2025, NAAC A+ accredited. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Chandigarh MBA specialisations. See the main Chandigarh Online MBA page for core subject list.

subjects.name: Strategy, Business and Globalization
subjects.desc: Supply chain strategy, global sourcing, and competitive positioning through logistics excellence.

subjects.name: Decision Science
subjects.desc: Quantitative techniques for inventory optimisation, route planning, and logistics network design.

subjects.name: Logistics Management
subjects.desc: Freight operations, multimodal transport, warehouse management systems, and 3PL relationships.

subjects.name: Quality Management and Quality Techniques
subjects.desc: ISO standards, six sigma basics, quality audits, and supplier quality management systems.

subjects.name: Logistics and Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: Supply chain integration, demand planning, inventory management, and SC risk mitigation.

subjects.name: AI-Enabled Demand Forecasting and Supply Chain Analytics
subjects.desc: ML-driven demand sensing, supply chain digital twin concepts, and AI-assisted inventory positioning.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: Project management for warehouse launches, SC transformation, and logistics network optimisation.

subjects.name: AI for Managers
subjects.desc: AI applications in fleet management, predictive maintenance, automated demand planning, and SC visibility.

subjects.name: Smart Logistics, Last-Mile and Quick Commerce Operations
subjects.desc: Hyperlocal logistics, Blinkit and Zepto delivery network design, last-mile optimisation, and dark store management.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.desc: Integrated operations management covering production, quality, and supply chain at the enterprise level.

subjects.name: Capstone Project II
subjects.desc: Applied logistics or supply chain project solving a real network, cost, or fulfilment challenge.

whoHires.body:
Logistics and Supply Chain MBA graduates from Chandigarh University Online are hired across India's logistics and supply chain ecosystem:

**Industries:** E-commerce logistics, 3PL companies, FMCG supply chains, pharmaceutical logistics, automotive supply chains, retail distribution, and government logistics

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics Coordinator, Procurement Executive, Warehouse Manager (junior), Demand Planner

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Manager, Distribution Network Manager, Category Head (supply chain)

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Supply Chain, Head of Logistics, Chief Procurement Officer, Head of Distribution, VP Operations

**Top hiring organisations:** Amazon Logistics, Flipkart Supply Chain, Delhivery, Blue Dart, DTDC, DHL India, Maersk India, Mahindra Logistics, HUL Supply Chain, ITC Logistics, Reliance Retail Distribution, Zomato Hyperpure, Blinkit (Zomato), and Swiggy Instamart supply chain

skills.body:
The Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation builds skills that logistics-intensive employers test at manager level:

**Technical skills:**
- Supply Chain Planning: demand forecasting methodologies, safety stock calculation, replenishment cycle design, and S&OP process facilitation
- Logistics Operations: warehouse layout optimisation, routing and scheduling, freight cost benchmarking, and 3PL SLA management
- AI in Logistics: demand sensing with ML models, last-mile algorithm basics, and supply chain visibility platform evaluation
- Quality Management: ISO 9001 documentation, supplier audit processes, process capability indices, and non-conformance management

**Applied skills:**
- Quick Commerce Operations: dark store design, hyperlocal zone management, SLA compliance at scale, and returns handling for q-comm
- Procurement: vendor evaluation frameworks, total cost of ownership analysis, contract terms, and supply risk assessment

**Soft skills:** Presenting supply chain KPIs to operations leadership, managing logistics vendor negotiations under service and cost pressure, building consensus on network design changes, and communicating supply chain risk to CFO and CEO audiences.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Chandigarh Online MBA LSCM against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA Operations and SCM:** Amity covers supply chain fundamentals well. CU's programme adds AI-Enabled Demand Forecasting and Smart Logistics with Quick Commerce operations - subjects Amity's current programme may not cover at this specificity. CU Tier 2 PwC Operations advisory content adds a consulting overlay. Fee: CU Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 vs Amity Rs 2,25,000.

**vs Manipal Jaipur Online MBA Supply Chain:** MUJ has NAAC A+ and a dedicated supply chain track. CU differentiates through NIRF #19, Quick Commerce operations, AI-enabled demand forecasting, and the PwC Supply Chain advisory partnership (Tier 2). For e-commerce logistics professionals, CU's curriculum is more specifically aligned with current industry practices.

**vs LPU Online MBA Operations/Supply Chain:** LPU is NAAC A++ at lower fee. CU's advantages are NIRF #19 ranking, more current AI and quick commerce curriculum, Harvard certificate, and PwC co-teaching (Tier 2). For senior logistics roles where credentials are checked, CU's profile is stronger.

fees.body:
The Chandigarh University Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management offers 2 program tiers based on certification bundle:

**Tier 1 (Primary) - Standard with Harvard ManageMentor Certificate**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000
- 25% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,65,000 net payable (Rs 55,000 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 82,500 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 41,250 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,65,000 one-time
- Included certification: Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Tier 2 (Premium Upgrade) - 3-Certificate Bundle**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000 (same base fee)
- 18% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,80,400 net payable (Rs 39,600 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 90,200 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 45,100 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,80,400 one-time
- Included certifications (3):
  1. PwC co-created and co-taught MBA modules (unmatched in India)
  2. CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management) certification preparation
  3. Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Additional charges (both tiers)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Chandigarh University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary, a differentiator among Indian online MBAs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options (6, 12, 18, or 24 months).

**Early Bird Scholarship terms**: Availability varies by admission cycle. The 25% Tier 1 scholarship and 18% Tier 2 scholarship are currently active but subject to timing. Reconfirm eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Chandigarh University each quarter. Please reconfirm current tier options, Early Bird eligibility, and fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Chandigarh University MBA degree certificate with Logistics and Supply Chain Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 standards. NIRF #19 and NAAC A+ credentials are recognised by logistics companies, e-commerce operators, and manufacturing supply chain employers.

Tier 1 graduates receive the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 graduates additionally receive PwC Supply Chain advisory co-teaching acknowledgement and CAPM certification preparation, strengthening profiles for supply chain consulting, network transformation, and logistics management roles. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Chandigarh University Online MBA Logistics and SCM alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Vishal K.
items.liked: Smart Logistics and Quick Commerce Operations subject directly applicable to my Blinkit supply chain role
items.disliked: AI Demand Forecasting module needed more hands-on Python lab component
items.body: I manage supply chain for a quick commerce dark store network. The Quick Commerce Operations subject was one of the most relevant MBA subjects I have encountered. Blinkit and Zepto supply chain case examples were accurate and practical. PwC supply chain advisory content (Tier 2) gave me a structured consulting framework for our network expansion project. Promoted to Supply Chain Manager.

items.name: Gurpreet S.
items.liked: NAAC A+ and NIRF #19 credential recognised in Punjab manufacturing sector
items.disliked: Procurement content could go deeper on strategic sourcing and contract law
items.body: The supply chain curriculum combined with NAAC A+ from NIRF #19 CU was the right combination for my manufacturing procurement career. AI-Enabled Demand Forecasting content helped me propose a machine learning forecasting pilot at work. Zero-cost EMI through the university made the Rs 1,65,000 investment very manageable. Good programme for logistics and supply chain professionals.

items.name: Preeti M.
items.liked: CAPM preparation alongside the MBA covered project management theory I needed
items.disliked: LMS had occasional technical issues during assignment submission windows
items.body: Chose Tier 2 for the CAPM and PwC combination. CAPM preparation covered PMI exam material systematically. Operations Management in Sem 4 gave me frameworks for my current 3PL management role. Harvard Business Publishing certificate was mentioned positively by my promotion committee. CU direct EMI worked well throughout the 24 months.

faqs.question: What is the PwC partnership in Chandigarh Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Chandigarh University's premium tier includes full MBA modules co-created and co-taught by PwC industry experts. This is unmatched among Indian online MBAs - most offer only generic curriculum. PwC-taught modules are particularly valuable for Logistics and Supply Chain professionals seeking roles at consulting firms, Big 4 operations advisory, or supply chain transformation practices at large companies. The PwC partnership is included in Tier 2 (premium) at Rs 1,80,400 net.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 fee options?
faqs.answer: Both tiers share the same Rs 2,20,000 base fee and identical curriculum. Tier 1 applies a 25% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,65,000 net with the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 applies an 18% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,80,400 net with three certifications: Harvard, CAPM preparation, and PwC co-taught modules. For SCM professionals targeting consulting or supply chain transformation roles, Tier 2 adds strong value.

faqs.question: Is zero-cost EMI really available directly through Chandigarh University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chandigarh University offers zero-cost EMI through the university's own payment infrastructure, not through an NBFC or external marketing partner. Tenures of 6, 12, 18, and 24 months are typically available. Confirm current options with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Does the MBA cover quick commerce and e-commerce logistics specifically?
faqs.answer: Yes. Smart Logistics, Last-Mile and Quick Commerce Operations in Sem 4 explicitly covers hyperlocal logistics, dark store management, Blinkit and Zepto-style delivery network design, and last-mile SLA compliance. This subject is among the most current in any Indian MBA programme and directly applicable to roles at Amazon Logistics, Flipkart, Blinkit, Swiggy Instamart, Zepto, and D2C brand logistics operations.

faqs.question: Is APICS CPIM or CSCP useful alongside this MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Chandigarh LSCM MBA covers management and strategic frameworks. APICS CPIM covers tactical operations in production and inventory management, while CSCP covers end-to-end supply chain strategy. Many supply chain managers hold both. The MBA gives institutional credibility; CPIM or CSCP gives professional supply chain certification that complements the degree for VP-level roles at FMCG and manufacturing companies.

chooseIf: You work in logistics, e-commerce supply chain, or manufacturing procurement and want a NIRF #19, NAAC A+ credential.
You want PwC Supply Chain advisory content and CAPM preparation (Tier 2) alongside your degree.
You are targeting quick commerce, last-mile, or AI-driven supply chain management roles.
You prefer zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: You need NMIMS or IIM brand specifically for Mumbai-based logistics consulting or FMCG supply chain.
You want dedicated APICS certification rather than an MBA in supply chain.
Your budget is under Rs 1.2 lakh and LPU NAAC A++ at lower fee is acceptable.
